definition: A deep outflow that keeps contamination, bondage, and continued becoming running on.
notes: The project keeps outflow as the default because it preserves the running-on and leaking image without importing archaic medical language. Taint and effluent remain controlled alternates when the contaminating side needs to be made more audible, but āsava should not collapse into generic defilement. In this repository, āsava belongs to the residue-and-bondage family as a deeper corruptive outflow, not to the actively obstructive nīvaraṇa layer, the latent anusaya layer, or the binding saṃyojana layer. Liberation formulas around āsavānaṃ khayā and āsavakkhaya should stay coordinated with this headword and should not rotate ad hoc among outflow, taint, effluent, and generic corruption language. The same policy extends by default into the named āsava compounds such as kāmāsava, bhavāsava, diṭṭhāsava, and avijjāsava unless a compound record documents a narrower override. The OSF glossary supports keeping outflow as the house default, and MN 2 and MN 36 support the standard release framing through the wearing away of the outflows.

context_rules.context: In most doctrinal and source-facing contexts
In explanatory prose emphasizing contamination rather than the flowing image
context_rules.notes: Allowed alternate when the contaminating force is more important than the image of running on.

context_rules.context: In translator-facing prose that needs a more technical English label without using archaic canker-language
context_rules.notes: Use selectively; it is not the house default.

context_rules.context: In liberation formulas such as āsavānaṃ khayā or āsavakkhaya
context_rules.notes: Keep the family wording stable across khaya and release contexts.

example_phrases.translation: through the wearing away of the outflows
example_phrases.notes: Widely recurring liberation formula. Keep the outflow-family wording stable here.

example_phrases.translation: the wearing away of the outflows
example_phrases.notes: Compact doctrinal compound for the same liberation goal.

example_phrases.pali: khīṇā jāti ... vusitaṃ brahmacariyaṃ ... nāparaṃ itthattāyāti pajānāti
example_phrases.translation: birth is worn away ... the holy life has been lived ... one discerns: there is no more of this state of being
example_phrases.notes: Standard arahant declaration that commonly appears alongside outflow-ending language and now aligns with the pajānāti policy.

authority_basis.scope: Supports keeping outflow as the house default while allowing taint and effluent only as controlled alternates.
Supports the liberation formulas āsavānaṃ khayā and āsavakkhaya.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'outflow' as the default rendering in the entry's governed doctrinal and liberation contexts unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Use 'taint' or 'effluent' only when the explanatory context clearly needs them. Do not flatten āsava into generic defilement or rotate among inherited English glosses inside one formula-family.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents drift between outflow-language, generic defilement-language, and older canker or fermentation renderings that obscure the repository's residue-family distinctions.
